title_guess: France: Laon, Vezelay, Burgundy, Puy-en-Velay, Conques, David's (Treignac) — summer tour

pages.text:
__Sunday__ We went for a walk to see the outside of a [?Pt] Priory but I had to turn back because I was tired. The others returned soon because the gate was locked.

    After lunch Simon is driving home in the van + Charles is taking Tinder + me on to Ansted where we will meet up with Kate + Lizzie, for Annette's cremation + funeral.

__Saturday July 25th__
__Just after 10 Simon & Eve took Tinder to kennels (£310 for 3 weeks) & set off to [?x x x x] Kent. V. Heavy traffic. Got to__ __Stalisfield__ __again. Great improvement in pub food under new owners. Just got a table at 6.30 pm. Need to book a meal ahead next year. As soon as we arrived I put the kettle on (5.10.) + then did yoga for 10 min + felt much less stiff.__

  __Sunday__ We set off at 8.45. & reached the Channel Tunnel car park at 9.10. An hour

early, but yesterday queues had been 5 hrs. 1/20 closed, Operation Stack to cope with waiting lorries. French trouble has been caused by both rioting asylum seekers + French tyre-burning [?for] ferry strikes.

   On towards Laon. The rain got [?x x] heavier & heavier. We reached Village [?Waten] at Suzy, a campsite with an American Rodeo/Wildwest Theme. Camping in wagons & tipis. Nice. We had a meal which had a good first course. Not good food really. Lots of girls who'd like horses.

Monday in the morning croissant for Simon + a baguette. No rain but overcast.
On to Laon, with the cows looking out. We parked + walked up lots of steps — perfect sized steps — the best I've ever walked up. Laon Cathedral is beautiful, especially the Tower of the crossing from inside! Built in 55 years. All of a piece. Laon was the Carolingian capital, on a hill, easy to defend. Walking along the streets is nice. Coffee

x x x  Etangs du Moulin

captions: Laon Cathedra built 1220 - 1264. Corners of towers cut away, so platforms for cows to look out.
pages.text: Laon Cathedra built 1220 - 1264. Corners of towers cut away, so platforms for cows to look out.

We drove thro the Laonnaise hills, skirting Champagne, then crossed a series of rivers such as Aisne, Marne + Seine on Droats. Stopped at a le Clerks. Reached Trainel Municipal Park -- rundown but clean.

Gorgeous chocolate
Tuesday  We had a lie in + set off to Vezelay by quite complicated main roads. At the carpark, halfway down the hill,

I had to wait while Simon went off to find the €6. We had lots of time to look at the west wall, 2 of them, inner + outer, + all the capitals. At one time 1000 pilgrims were burned when the cathedral caught fire. It was involved with crusades. Richard the [lion]heart visited.

    We started with lunch + I had chocolate waffles gou[?]ffles, for pudding.
*****
    On to Chaumot, near Corbigny run by a very relaxed Dutchman. He bought a station house near here 25 years ago + this campsite 14 years ago. The loos are brilliant – easy to clean – loo, bin, brush off the floor. It's on Canal Nivernais, so we can bike ride tomorrow. It started to rain when I was in the loo + has gone on + on. Sodden cyclists keep arriving...
Wednesday July 29th.

We are on a sort of island with a bridge at one end. There was a railway here once which interchanged with the canal. The next village has "mines" in

the name - silver mine. More rain in the night. The washing machine is in the men's wash area. A nice man helped me by plugging it in. Only 35 minutes - just what u need for a camp site. When we arrived the levelling block kept bouncing back as Simon tried to ride up it + the kind Dutchman came out with a peg (ours were not to hand because no children ∴ no tent.)

	Campsite owners save themselves a lot of trouble by not having pitches. The rule here is "one night cycle tents" in the middle - everyone else around the outside." I do like Dutch-owned camp sites.
I hung the washing in the van. It rained most of the morning + stopped about noon. After lunch we set off northwards past Dirol to [?Monceau]. For part of the journey we were taken off the tow path + onto back lanes. A lovely ride about 12m. At [?Monceau] we bought pate + a tart.
	There are cows in the field next to us. Nice Dutch neighbours. Our neighbour is a family friend of the owner. He comes at the beginning of summer to help set up the camp (not plumbing) electrics, lawnmow[er]

+ he + his wife can stay on for free. He's on site to mend things, including children's toys - go-kart. His wife has a zimmer.
	They tell us about a concert about to happen on the 3rd night after our arrival at which people can join in. He sings a Dutch song.
	__Thursday__ Day of concert. We take lunch with us on a bike ride. My knees hurt every pedal by the end of the ride. V. painful getting in + out of van. 12 miles, past potter we visited years before up light - staircase - of 16 locks. We reach the top + Simon goes 2k on the other side to see our old campsite. There are a boy + girl at abt of the locks, manning the gates. Boaters are not given their own paddles. Nivernais built float logs for Paris firewood, 1784 - 1835.
Back at the camp we go to the concert 9 - midnight. V. cold. Nearly everyone else went off to bed before the end. We wore socks, shawls + blankets. Wonderful boogie playing by a 17 yr old. + another brilliant pianist + super singer, older + fatter than I am but a marvellous voice. And a good drummer + blond singer. Beatles, Dutch, French songs. Imagine, Bob Dylan.
Corrine, singer, part owner of camp w/ Reynard, Dutchman

captions: Paray-le-Monial (Saône-et-Loire) Burgundy Christ in Glory, North Portal, [?Crche]
pages.text:
Friday we set off about 9.45 or 10, after filling w/ water + emptying loo.

Paray-le-Monial

[three photos of church/abbey interior]

Paray-le-Monial (Saône-et-Loire) Burgundy
Christ in Glory, North Portal, [?Crche]

off to Intermarché, [?Mouskatère], better than he Clerke earlier in the week. Rabbit pâté good. We had lunch on a Canal, le Canal lateral de la Loire, next to an Abbey. On to Paray-le-Monial. This has many women's religious orders represented. Simon didn't come into one of the most interesting churches with this theme because it had a "no shorts" sign. The main Abbeychurch has lovely [?Cotswold] stone. No nave, designed by Hugo from Cluny who was influenced by Mozarabic in Spain. A well-kept old town. We cycled back to the campsite. A boys' camp packed up. Lots more Dutch. Cows. I looked in a cabin while it was being cleaned. The campsite felt airless. During the night it rained + rained.
Saturday August 1st.
It still rained in the morning. 10.30. We set off + had elevenses at the Abbey of Dieu Donné Abbaye [?Bènisitions] Dieu, gorgeous steeple + roof of Beaune, coloured tiles. Benedictine. 1135

laures Fracasso's father  03-85-84-05-22
Laure was our au pair about 22 years ago. Her family (1 real sister + 7 adopted siblings) her parents came to visit for a w/end then. Both Laure + her father have hare-lips.
   When we get near where they used to live, I got Simon to stop the van. I went into the nearest shop - a sit-on lawnmower shop. I said "Do you know the Fracasso family?" + the man pointed to his lip, which was also a hare lip, & said "Like this?" + I said "yes".

So we now have [?the] Fracasso's [tel.] no. It was 77 amazing.
   We reached St. Maurice + looked all around the village - a tower + a church w/ frescoes. V. steep streets. Views of the damned lake. Then we motored on ... + on ... + on. Nearly or 3 hours, trying to find campsite. We have now reached a v. entertaining one, bursting with noisy children. On a lake, fishing, swimming, badminton, bikes, skate boards. All young, whereas Chaumont was mostly old. [We] are parked opposite the Loos, camouflaged with bamboos + we see parents + children coming + going. There's a deli + soapseller. We buy chips from the bar. A good end to the day. __Arpheuilles__ at __St. Paul de Vézelin__. (v. good for families)
__Sunday__ There were 2 layers of mist on the reservoir. It's lovely + [warm] today - the first day we've had breakfast outside. We drove off from the Gorges of the Loire south. Bigger hills, [Saw] past St Etienne. Simon bought lunch in St. Maurice de Lignon + stopped soon after in a village ringed round a huge

pages.sketch_desc: Printed map (part of glued-in leaflet) showing the Greenway route alongside La Loire river; labels include La Loire, Valence, Brives-Charensac (Old station, Campsite Brives), and Latour. Route runs north–south with a green line indicating the cycleway.
pages.text:
green. Men are dismantling a village marquee after yesterday's festival.  __La Besse__

On to __Puy en Velay__, campsite 3 k away at Brives Charensac. Yerghot.
__Monday__ At 10 we left on the bike ride. Brives → Solignac. Arrived at 1 + had picnic lunch. Every pedal + push, steady incline. Only 1½ hours to get back, free-wheeling nearly all the way. The cycleway surface is formed from chippings laid on the original railway ballast and generally easy. The tunnels are well lit with occasional places where water falls from the roof. We saw no food at Solignac.

[printed leaflet glued in]
__HISTORY:__
The railway line from Le Puy-en-Velay to Langogne came into service in 1912 to provide a link between the industrial centres of the St Etienne region and those of the Cévennes.
However, since 1981, trains have deserted this route and the rails have been removed to create a pleasant path which attracts walkers, cyclists and mountain bikers.

__DESCRIPTION:__
The starting point of the Greenway (North–South direction) is situated 4 km from Le Puy-en-Velay on the way out of Brives Charensac station (altitude 600 m). A gentle climb (2%), easy for all, takes you to Solignac-sur-Loire (altitude 850 m). The path goes through 5 tunnels, all lit up, and a metal-spanned viaduct, 184 m long.

pages.sketch_desc: Continuation of printed map from left page showing the full Greenway route. Runs from Solignac/Loire (alt. 857 m) at top through tunnels (Veneyres, Pradeaux, Malpas, Riou, Taulhac) and stations (Coubon-Yolhac) south to Le Puy-en-Velay. Arrows and km markers indicate direction and distances. Practical information legend on right side.
pages.text:
[handwritten text top-left, partially obscured by map]
[?We] [?restaurant] + [?washed] [?the] [?by] [?hands]. We ate at the camp restaurant. Good lasagna. Sorbet, [?and] vanilla ice cream + [?had] caramel ice cream + chocolate sauce

[printed leaflet/map continuation]
[Map labels:] Solignac/Loire, Old station Solignac/Loire alt. 857 m, Km 13.8, Aubenas, Mende, Gagne viaduct, Tunnel de Veneyres, __Veneyres__ Km 12.1, Tunnel de Pradeaux, Malpas alt. 858 m, Tunnel de Malpas, [?Coubon], Cussac-sur-Loire alt. 651 m, Old station __Coubon-Yolhac__ alt. 720 m, Km 4.9, Latour Km 3.8, Tunnel de Riou, Le Riou Km 8.6, Tunnel de Taulhac, Le Puy-en-Velay alt. 621 m, St-Etienne, Lyon

[Practical information box:]
Greenway —
Main access point
Secondary access point
Nearby town or village
Bicycle hire
No access for motorized vehicles
Emergency telephone number +
Eat before feeling hungry and drink before feling thirsty
Remember that the tunnels may be cold
Respect the Greenway, the environment and surrounding property
Keep dogs on leads

captions: Aiguille rock seen from the banks of the Borne
pages.text:
Aiguille rock seen from the banks of the Borne

I [?rea] remember seeing the allotments from the rock on our last visit.

__Monday~Tuesday__
 It was so hot that it should have rained heavily in the night, with thunderstorms, but it didn't. There was a bit of rain in the morning. We cycled along the Voie Verte for 5 km to [?Auys] thro suburbs & past the view above. We climbed up to see the cathedral. You walk up the hill, then there are 60 steps until you reach the front of the building, then up another 75 steps to level with the top of the arch * – the walker comes out almost under the crossing at the front of the nave – very unusual.
	 I bought lace & we walked around the town steep cobbles & had a good lunch. The buildings are 5 stories high. It [?is] a rich town.

captions: Below the Holy Cross (15th century) lies the 'apparition' stone which is said to be part of the sick sanctuary. According to legend, in a dream, a sick woman received the order to climb the mount. When she reached the top, she noticed a flat stone where she lay down and fell asleep. Around her gathered "a multitude of holy and saints, and above them, a woman of royal dignity and saints, and above her clothes, everything about her was majesty; her face, the woman of royal dignity about her was extraordinary beauty". When she awoke, she was healed. Today,
pages.text:
[Two photographs glued in. Top photo shows a town square with a large Romanesque cathedral facade, crowds of people, and tall stone buildings — likely Le Puy-en-Velay. Bottom photo shows a recumbent stone statue of a figure, with a printed caption card attached:]

Below the Holy Cross (15th century) lies the 'apparition' stone which is said to be part of the sick sanctuary. According to legend, in a dream, a sick woman received the order to climb the mount. When she reached the top, she noticed a flat stone where she lay down and fell asleep. Around her gathered "a multitude of holy and saints, and above them, a woman of royal dignity and saints, and above her clothes, everything about her was majesty; her face, the woman of royal dignity about her was extraordinary beauty". When she awoke, she was healed. Today,

This is another good campsite for families. Swimming pool & cycling. At 9.30, pm there are 26 children in the playground & field.

~~Tuesday~~ Wednesday
We motored south west & I got very very very hot in the passengers seat. We had sausages in Grandvieu, a rather sad town where most of the shops were closed - either the owners were on holiday or they were being sold, [?+]. Most odd mannkin/puppets all over. __Valde[?]__ [?Saures]. Spooky. We reached __Entrayques__. The [?campsite] computer knew that we'd been here 2006 + 2010. The [?last] 2006 visit was when Simon & Charles spent hours playing with a plastic bottle & stick in the rapids. I really like Ent... the flat level bridge takes you [?to] right into town. Late, about 8.30, we walked up into town & saw trestle tables & lots of people eating in the square & local produce on sale.

__Thursday__
A lovely day, reading all day, Simon a book on Turing & I a Clancy whodunnit. I did a 9 kilo wash, everything, that was dry by lunch time. A father & son cycling duo wearing orange & black are in the [?pitch] [?happened]

pages.sketch_desc: A simple hand-drawn sketch of a bridge, showing the upstream elevation with pointed arch buttresses and the downstream side with square towers (or bases). Labeled 'upstream' above the sketch with an arrow. The number '13' appears in a circle beside the sketch, appearing to be an annotation reference.
pages.text:
I don't know how they do it. I'm too hot lying in the shade.

Before supper we walked on the island that gets flooded, & admired all the trees. After we went to bed there was super disco music for dancing until very late.... but Simon was snoring.

Friday We have been getting up later & later which is nice. After breakfast we walked up to the market in town & bought food, bought a girl's dress to copy for the 5 granddaughters. We walked to the 'old Bridge' [sketch of bridge with label: upstream] (13). It is a magnificent building with pointed buttresses on the upstream side & square towers downstream, or the bases for [what were] square towers. It has withstood snow & flood for 700 years.
The oldest street in town is hideously decrepit with rotting buildings for sale, which may well be flooded at times.
At tea time we invited an Irishman from the

captions: Abbatiale Sainte-Foy, tympan du Jugement dernier (début du XIIe siècle)
pages.text:
opposite pitch. He & his son are cycling St. Clau[?]hyons. He's off to Nepal in the [crossed out] winter.

We chose a restaurant on the square which advertised puddings made in the restaurant. It was a very good choice. Samu[el] had tripe + [Alison?]. I had salmon pasta. Samin[?] had chef's pastry for [just?] – as adored by me + I had choc. [Sou?] [Fondant?]. Both delicious.

Thunderstorms in the night. V.v. hot. The pharmaciene at the next table (with 2 her sons) was V. nice + invited us to look her up at the pharmacy on [bar?] our next visit. She knows local restaurants.

Saturday

Simon bought cakes for the family next door. V. well behaved. We left.

[Beside photo:] In Conques we ate at the Ch[ar?][magne?] rest[aurant?] [Alyst?] salads; had [chestnut?] crème [brulee?] good coffee. It [rained?] on + off [all] day. We walk[ed] a[round] the town enjoyed looking at [illegible]

captions: C'est à un voyage privilégié dans l'étage des tribunes de l'abbatiale que le visiteur est exceptionnellement convié depuis peu. Il y découvrira des perspectives inoubliables et percevra, par les jeux d'éclairage, la beauté plastique des chapiteaux romans.
pages.text:
the old buildings + shops. We drove on to [?] race thru hills + along [?] + rain. We had trouble remembering [where] the campsite [is]. It was no longer advertising camping [when] we got there.

White monks organised a short service in the abbey with psalms at noon. The metal work grills around the sanctuary were made of prisoners' chains. St. foy was prayed to for prisoners. The abbey has continued this theme by having writing + photos about hostages, torture + prisoners Today, since 1995, in Eritraya. 10,000 people have disappeared. Enforced national service is in a brutal army

Sunday

Rain in the night. Damp but no rain in the morning. Apparently the rain is the first they've had since June 10th.

We drove to Figeac, looked at the Rosetta Stone, had good coffee in the covered market & shopped. At 12 we arrived at John Hurman's & met his freind, Margaret Tucker. We had lunch on his Terrace, but, again (a (5 years ago) we had to move in because of rain during the meal. It was a lovely afternoon. We ended with playing Skittles. In the evening we went along old roads around __Cajarc Campsite__ **** There is a pool, a lovely walnut orchard, but the loo block is v old.
Monday
__Simon has found that 2 of the 4 brackets holding up the water tanks have slackened__, so after about 130 miles we found land with suitable hollows & Simon has parked the van over them & is under the van working on the brackets. We went to a supermarket & on towards David's. At Chambert we again stopped to buy glue to mend a broken drawer handle. We arrived & were made to feel very welcome. David & Sally like the Taj Mahal table. We parked the van overlooking the stupendous view from the bottom of the garden, away from the trees because there may be a storm. We played whist & looked at the stars. Sally has read there should be lots of

shooting stars for 3 days. Simon saw 3, I saw 1.
__Tuesday__
We set off about 10.20. for a walk to Treignac - Sally, Helen, Simon & I. On the way I had to ask at a house for water. At 12.45. we got there. David arrived with Maia & Orla & Jean & bikes. We had crepes & beer & swam in the lake. Simon & Jean biked home, the rest of us drove. We got very worried when Simon didn't arrive on the Brompton (David's 5 years) it had a puncture. We had lunch & Sally read a book. Simon & David mended things. I carted horse manure thro the barn to David's trees in the other field. David cooked risotto for supper.
__Wednesday__
Some went shopping. Maia & I took

Orla [?up] to see the white horses from the Camargue. The farmer arrived + we had a long chat. I was nervous with the horses so near. Orla failed to sleep.
After lunch everyone went off to swim to [?storm] lake Viam , with Jem cycling. I stayed behind + read + did more [?manure] + washing. Simon, David, Helen, Eve went to __Thursday__ a guitar concert in a church at 8.30. It was desperately hot, with no through draught. The east end of the church was wooden, painted with pilasters + niches to look classical. The ceiling was wooden barrel. Lovely music by Alain Rizoul at Rilhac Treignac, but way too hot.
    We are on the Plateau des Mille Vaches, which means plateau of a thousand villages, not a thousand cows.
    Simon has moved the van up to the front door so that we don't get stuck.
__Thursday__
Rain + storms in the night. We have to go to

bed in the dark. At about 11 enormous hornets come out of their nest in the tree + fly to the kitchen lights.
    Simon + Eve go to Intermarché. Adam arrives. Simon, Eve, Helen, David, Sally go for a walk in the afternoon + pick blackberries + get caught, just, in a thunderstorm. A lovely walk.
    We all went out to supper at Masseret. It was delicious food, even the cheese, + a good waiter. Simon's sides fell off under the table, he passed out in just shoe uppers.
__Friday__ more rain.
    9:15. after David had signed some papers we left. We bought bread at Chamboret, (where Simon revved up forward into a hidden bollard) + left, with Helen, for the motorway. Apart from my driving for 1½ hrs, Simon drove the whole way until we reached the Hermitage Hotel, Montreuil, 8.45 pm. At which point Simon asked Helen if she had her passport. She said she didn't, then she said she did + produced a brown envelope. When Simon opened it there was only her old passport inside it. Simon telephoned David who said a very rude word. He has now texted Simon all Helen's passport details.
